$comment: Release self-declaration consumed by the unified registry's auto-core-release resolver (sh2-plugin-registry/scripts/resolve-core-candidate.mjs) at the git tag being released. The supports.* semver ranges decide whether a tagged core can be auto-matched with the latest published counterparts into a signed release candidate PR. Keep pluginApiVersion in sync with config/services.yaml (CI enforces it) and widen/bump supports.frontend deliberately - while the platform is pre-1.0, every 0.x minor is breaking. Raise supports.frontend's lower bound whenever core changes/removes an API contract the frontend depends on. Floor 0.1.30 is the first frontend that BOTH (a) adopts the field-naming unification - migration Version20260622165615 drops the `shared_` prefix from 47 style props (`shared_color`->`color`, `shared_variant`->`variant`, `shared_radius`->`radius`, ...; reserved `shared_height`/`shared_width`/`shared_icon` keep their prefix), so this core emits the unprefixed names a 0.1.23-0.1.28 frontend does not read - AND (b) handles the core 0.1.18 anonymous-access hardening: an anonymous `preview=true` is now rejected with 401, so frontend 0.1.30 gates SSR preview on a live session cookie and clears `sh_preview` on logout/expiry (mobile 0.1.10 ships the matching previewPolicy gate). A 0.1.29 frontend left a stale admin preview cookie 401-looping the public site against this core, so the floor moved 0.1.29 -> 0.1.30. The 0.1.30 floor still carries everything the previous floors required - the full 0.1.15 style-schema contract (mobile-rendering taxonomy: style `renderTarget`, required field `scope`, the unprefixed-common/web_/mobile_ field split with `pages.id_platform` removed in favour of the page access type) PLUS the alert/badge/avatar/button/login authoring-polish wave - so an older frontend can never be auto-assembled with this core into a release. The resolver refuses any incompatible frontend+backend pair in either direction. Core 0.1.20 adds the CMS-driven mobile-preview install/update endpoints (/cms-api/v1/admin/system/update/mobile-preview/*) and the `mobile_preview_version` field on /admin/system/version; both are ADDITIVE and optional, so supports.frontend stays >=0.1.30 (the core does not require the preview-update UI - frontend 0.1.32 adopts it and raises ITS supports.core floor to 0.1.20). Core 0.1.21 adds the `admin.mobile_preview.view` permission (migration Version20260623193630) gating the frontend's full-screen Live Preview surface; it is ADDITIVE (no new route, no schema change - the live preview reuses the existing mint+exchange routes), so supports.frontend stays >=0.1.30 (frontend 0.1.33 adopts the live preview and raises ITS supports.core floor to 0.1.21). Core 0.1.23 implements the immutable data-column contract (host issue #56): data_cols gains an immutable field_key + mutable display_name (+display_name_source), the admin columns endpoint now returns {id, fieldKey, displayName} instead of {id, name}, the interpolation variables move out of the getSection payload into a dedicated GET /cms-api/v1/admin/sections/{section_id}/data-variables endpoint (a token => label map), and a new PATCH /cms-api/v1/admin/data/tables/{tableName}/columns/display-name (permission admin.data.update_columns) curates labels. That is a BREAKING response-shape change the frontend consumes, so supports.frontend is raised 0.1.30 -> 0.1.48 (frontend 0.1.48 reads the new column shape + fetches the token/label picker endpoint and raises ITS supports.core floor to 0.1.23). Core 0.1.24 is an ADDITIVE issue-#56 follow-up: renaming a CMS form input now propagates its name to the immutable section_<id> column's auto label on save; data_tables gains the same auto|manual provenance FK (id_display_name_source, migration Version20260629074004) with a new PATCH /cms-api/v1/admin/data/tables/{tableName}/display-name (permission admin.data.update_tables, migration Version20260629074552) to rename+lock a table; and a `locked` flag is added to the tables list + columns list while a form section's getSection payload gains a data_table {id,name,display_name,locked} block for the inspector badge/deep-link. All additions are backward-compatible extra fields + a new route, so supports.frontend stays >=0.1.48 (frontend 0.1.49 adopts the lock UI + deep link and raises ITS supports.core floor to 0.1.24). Core 0.1.25 is the Interpolation v2 contract switch (host issue #56): the `{{ }}` editor and the render-time `retrieved_data` scope now resolve data variables by the immutable `field_key` instead of the mutable input name, so the section `data-variables` map becomes `{{scope.field_key}} => display_name` and a show-user-input section's payload gains a `field_labels` (`field_key` => `display_name`) header map with `entries` kept keyed by `field_key`. That is a BREAKING render/contract change the frontend consumes (label chips that store `{{field_key}}` + show-user-input headers that default to `display_name`), so supports.frontend is raised 0.1.48 -> 0.1.52 (frontend 0.1.52 adopts the chip/label round-trip + show-user-input header map and raises ITS supports.core floor to 0.1.25). No data migration ships; pre-release `{{scope.name}}` content is recreated against the new field_key picker. Core 0.1.26 is an ADDITIVE Interpolation v2 follow-up: a single context-aware endpoint GET /cms-api/v1/admin/interpolation/variables (route admin_interpolation_variables_get, permission admin.page.read, migration Version20260629110606) now serves the `{{ }}` picker catalog for any context (section|page|action|global via InterpolationVariableService over DataVariableResolver's per-context catalogs), the seeded auth mail templates move onto the shared system.* namespace ({{system.user_name}}/{{system.user_code}} + one-time {{system.special.activation_link|reset_link|platform_link}}; MailTemplateService maps the flat caller vars onto that nested context, legacy flat tokens still resolve), and the action picker offers recipient.*/record.<field_key>/system.*. The new route is additive and the section data-variables route still exists, so an older frontend keeps working and supports.frontend stays >=0.1.52; frontend 0.1.53 adopts the unified endpoint across page/action/css/json surfaces and raises ITS supports.core floor to 0.1.26. Core 0.1.27 is the type-driven-editor follow-up (host issue #56): the admin columns endpoint now PREPENDS the always-present projection columns (id/id_users/user_name/record_id/entry_date/id_users_deleted) flagged standard:true/id:null/locked:true, and migration Version20260629143116 retypes overloaded fields so the editor is chosen purely from the field type (7 structured-config blobs textarea->json; html_tag_content textarea->code; the short message fields error_text/empty_text/loading_text/confirm_message/delete_modal_body text->textarea). Both are response/behavior changes the frontend renders (a pre-0.1.54 frontend would mis-render the standard columns and the new `code` type), so supports.frontend is raised 0.1.52 -> 0.1.54 (frontend 0.1.54 renders the standard columns + the type-driven editors and raises ITS supports.core floor to 0.1.27). Mail bodies also move to WYSIWYG rich-text fragments rendered at send time by MailHtmlRenderer (internal; no API contract change).
